effective_here: Current admitted R8 memory streams remain precision-stable on 4/4 screened streams under the bounded grid.
unproven_here: 0/4 screened streams need decomposition recovery or remain boundary-bearing, so broader robustness is still unproven.
negated_here: Single-head float32 fails immediately without screened recovery on 0/4 admitted R8 memory streams, and the weaker base-256 control fails on 0/1 boundary-bearing follow-ups.

supported_here: R9 stays companion-only on exact-admitted R8 rows and sharpens the current real-trace precision boundary without changing endpoint scope.
The bounded grid distinguishes streams that remain stable at default settings from streams that only recover under a bounded decomposition follow-up.
unsupported_here: R9 does not justify a broader stack-wide or unseen-family precision guarantee.
R9 does not reopen arbitrary compiled-language claims or widen the D0 endpoint.
